records.ety: [OF. contendre, L. contendere, -tentum; con- + tendere to strech. See Tend.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contended; p. pr. & vb. n. Contending.]

defs: To strive in opposition; to contest; to dispute; to vie; to quarrel; to fight.
quotes: For never two such kingdoms did contend Without much fall of blood.
The Lord said unto me, Distress not the Moabites, neither contend with them in battle.
In ambitious strength I did Contend against thy valor.

defs: To struggle or exert one's self to obtain or retain possession of, or to defend.
quotes: You sit above, and see vain men below Contend for what you only can bestow.

defs: To strive in debate; to engage in discussion; to dispute; to argue.
quotes: The question which our author would contend for.
Many things he fiercely contended about were trivial.
Carthage shall contend the world with Rome.Dryden.
